summary: A superb soundboard capture of the Amsterdam 5/10/72 show with crystal‑clear mix and a legendary, fire‑breathing rendition of “The Other One”.
review: This recording delivers an excellent soundboard experience – crisp, well‑balanced and only faint tape hiss in the quiet moments. Musically the concert is highlighted by an explosive second set (Truckin' → The Other One → Me & Bobby McGee → Wharf Rat) and a powerhouse “The Other One” that many reviewers call one of the best ever. While the first set has a few rough edges, the overall performance is energetic, tight, and unmistakably one of Europe ’72’s top shows.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive reviews praise both the musical performance (especially The Other One) and the high‑quality soundboard mix; minor tape or first‑set quirks keep it just short of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with excellent instrument separation; slight tape hiss on quiet passages and occasional minor speed/tape‑speed questions noted by a few reviewers, but overall superb fidelity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is solid but uneven, while the second set shines with a seamless Truckin' → The Other One → Me & Bobby McGee → Wharf Rat sequence; overall pacing keeps the energy high throughout the marathon 4¼‑hour concert.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s solos are repeatedly praised – emotive on El Paso, fiery throughout The Other One, and generally “perfect” or “highly expressive”.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass work is called “amazing” and a key driver of the expansive jams, especially during The Other One.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir receives few specific notes; occasional vocal lead on Jack Straw mentioned but otherwise not highlighted.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s keyboards earn compliments for superb separation and contribution to the jam sections (He’s Gone, The Other One).
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum work is lauded for propelling the long improvisations, with particular shout‑outs during The Other One and the second‑set sequence.
